geopolitics_summary: EU-China trade tensions rising amid sanctions on Russian-linked firms; Trump tariff threats strain US-EU trade truce; energy demand surge from industry/AI strains grids.

themes.headline: EU legislation targets peak-demand reduction via AI as industrial electricity demand surges
themes.why_it_matters: Demand-side flexibility via AI targets peak shaving; reduces need for expensive marginal generation and grid reinforcement. Direct impact on DA spread and intraday power price volatility. Peak demand management lowers Cal+1 volatility.

themes.headline: Strait of Hormuz closure drives U.S. jet fuel to record high production; crude prices elevated
themes.why_it_matters: Strait closure (Feb 28) triggered crude price spike and supply concerns in Europe/Asia. Higher crude increases marginal fuel-switching cost toward gas in power generation, supporting EU power prices and gas demand. TTF risk via European power margin compression.

themes.headline: EU sanctions Chinese firms aiding Russia, escalating Brussels-Beijing tensions
themes.why_it_matters: Sanctions escalation against Chinese-Russian cooperation may constrain critical supply chains (semiconductors, energy infra components). Indirect: lengthens grid modernisation timelines, pressures renewables deployment velocity, raises capex for EU power sector. Months-forward structural risk.

themes.headline: EU trade chief proposes new diversification tool to reduce China supply dependence
themes.why_it_matters: Mandatory supply diversification in critical sectors (including energy equipment, renewables components). Increases procurement friction and costs; delays infrastructure deployment. Indirect upward pressure on power capex, widening generation costs and supporting longer-term power curve.

themes.headline: Global shipping industry adapts to permanent disruption; rising geopolitical fragmentation
themes.why_it_matters: Shipping fragmentation raises LNG logistics costs and delays to Europe. Higher LNG transport cost narrows US-to-EU arb, tightens TTF supply, supports gas and coal-fired power economics. Weeks-to-months horizon for LNG delivery timing risk.

watchlist: EU forced labour tariff negotiation outcome with Trump administration; truce fragility.
Straits geopolitics: continued supply disruption risk and crude/energy price contagion to EU.
EU AI-driven demand-side management rollout timeline and grid impact studies.
Chinese sanctions escalation and critical component supply chain bottlenecks for renewables/grid.
